How We Vet Every Tradesperson

No one gets through without passing.

Before a tradesperson can quote on your job, they pass a rigorous multi-layer verification that checks identity, credentials, insurance, criminal record and track record — automatically, in real time.

The 9 Checks

Every layer, explained

Nine independent checks run concurrently — meaning a single failure anywhere in the chain rejects the application entirely.

Check 01
Identity Verification
Government-issued photo ID matched against HMRC and Companies House records. Every applicant, no exceptions.
Check 02
Trade Licence Check
Live API queries to Gas Safe, NICEIC, NAPIT, OFTEC and 12 other regulatory bodies. Status confirmed in seconds.
Check 03
Insurance Validation
Public liability minimum ÂŁ2M verified via live insurer document scan. Cover must be current and uninterrupted.
Check 04
DBS Criminal Check
Standard DBS mandatory for all trades. Enhanced DBS required for tradespeople entering homes with vulnerable occupants.
Check 05
Competence Card
CSCS, SkillCard or equivalent verified for all site and structural categories. NVQ Level 2 minimum for wet trades.
Check 06
Portfolio Review
Our team manually reviews a sample of past project photos. Work quality, finish standard and complexity are all assessed.
Check 07
Reference Validation
We contact a minimum of two previous clients directly to confirm work quality, timekeeping and professional conduct.
Check 08
Right to Work in UK
Biometric or documentary evidence of legal right to work checked in line with Home Office guidelines. No exemptions.
Check 09
Annual Re-Verification
All credentials are automatically re-checked every 12 months. A lapsed licence or expired insurance triggers immediate suspension.
Databases cross-checked
15+
HMRC, Companies House & more
Match accuracy
99.2%
Automated ID scoring
1
Identity & Right to Work

We know exactly who you are.

Every applicant submits government-issued photo ID — passport or driving licence — which is verified in real time against a biometric scoring engine. We cross-reference against HMRC self-employment records and Companies House simultaneously.

Biometric document scan
NFC chip reading on e-passports for highest-grade verification.
Right to Work check
Full Home Office-compliant right-to-work assessment. Mandatory for every applicant without exception.
Fraud database screening
Names and national insurance numbers checked against CIFAS fraud prevention registers.
Regulatory bodies
15
APIs queried live
2
Licence & Accreditation

Credentials that actually check out.

We don't accept uploaded certificates. We call the register. Our platform sends live API queries to 15+ regulatory bodies — Gas Safe, NICEIC, NAPIT, OFTEC, ELECSA, WaterSafe, FMB and more — and confirms active, unexpired registration in real time.

Live register queries
Zero tolerance for screenshot submissions. Status pulled directly from source.
Expiry date monitoring
Tradesperson and homeowner both notified automatically 30 days before any credential expires.
CSCS card verification
Construction Skills Certification Scheme checked for all structural, roofing and groundwork trades.
Minimum cover accepted
ÂŁ2M
Public liability insurance
3
Insurance & Liability

If anything goes wrong, you're covered.

All tradespeople must hold valid public liability insurance — minimum ÂŁ2M — before joining. We scan the certificate document, verify the insurer directly, and confirm cover is both current and adequate for the category of work being performed.

Document OCR scan
Certificate text extracted and validated. Cover amounts, dates and named insured all checked.
Tiered minimums by trade
Structural builders require ÂŁ5M. Gas engineers require ÂŁ2M. Handymen and decorators require ÂŁ1M minimum.
Void project protection
If insurance lapses mid-project, payment is frozen until cover is reinstated or a dispute is resolved.
Enhanced DBS
Req'd
For vulnerable households
4
DBS & Criminal Record

Your home, your safety.

A Standard DBS check is mandatory for every tradesperson on the platform. Those who work in homes where elderly, disabled or otherwise vulnerable people reside must provide an Enhanced DBS check — no exceptions and no workarounds.

Standard DBS — all trades
Checked against Police National Computer, covering convictions, cautions and reprimands.
Enhanced DBS — vulnerable households
Full barred-list check against both the Adults' and Children's Barred Lists held by the DBS.
Periodic refresh
DBS checks are renewed every 3 years or immediately upon any reported incident on platform.
Application Journey

From application to approved

Here's exactly what happens from the moment a tradesperson submits their application to the moment they go live on the platform.

âś“ Verified & Live
01
Step 1
Application Submitted
Tradesperson completes online form with personal details, trade category, upload of all credentials and consent for all checks.
Online form Document upload Consent given
02
Step 2
Automated Checks Run
All API-based checks fire immediately: identity scoring, licence register queries, insurance OCR, right-to-work verification and CIFAS fraud check.
Avg. 4 minutes 15 APIs called Any failure = instant reject
03
Step 3
DBS Check Initiated
Standard or Enhanced DBS check submitted to the Disclosure and Barring Service. Typical turnaround 24–72 hours.
24–72h DBS turnaround PNC checked
04
Step 4
Manual Portfolio Review
Our team personally reviews project photos and assesses finish quality, complexity and trade standards against category benchmarks.
Human review Min. 5 project photos
05
Step 5
Reference Validation
We call or email a minimum of two previous clients directly to verify the quality of work, reliability and professional conduct of the applicant.
2+ references required Direct contact only
06
Final Step
Approved & Goes Live
All checks passed. Profile published, Verified badge awarded, and the tradesperson can now receive and bid on relevant jobs immediately.
Avg. 18h total time Badge issued Bidding enabled
Ongoing — Not One-Off

Verification never stops.

Passing the initial check is just the beginning. Our monitoring systems run continuously, ensuring every tradesperson on the platform stays verified every single day.

Annual full re-verification
Every credential is re-checked in full every 12 months, automatically — no action required from the tradesperson.
30-day expiry alerts
Tradesperson notified 30 days before any insurance or licence expiry. Failure to renew triggers automatic suspension.
Incident-triggered review
Any complaint or dispute of sufficient severity triggers an immediate manual review of the tradesperson's standing.
Live register polling
Gas Safe, NICEIC and NAPIT registers are polled daily. A removed registration triggers same-day suspension.

4.2%
Rejection rate — our bar stays high, always
Why We Reject

High standards mean real rejections.

We reject one in twenty-four applicants. Not because of technicalities — because they genuinely don't meet the standard we hold our tradespeople to. Here are the most common reasons.

  • Invalid or lapsed trade licence — the most common reason. The register says no, we say no.
  • Insurance below the minimum — cover exists but doesn't meet our tiered minimums for the trade category.
  • DBS disclosure — convictions or cautions relevant to working in domestic properties.
  • Portfolio below standard — work quality assessed as insufficient for the claimed trade and category level.
  • Reference failed — previous client unable to confirm work quality or reported serious concerns.
  • Identity mismatch — documents provided don't match the individual, or CIFAS fraud flag detected.
